prompt:
Create a SecureRoom Brainstorm Studio for offline product, design, and ops teams to run high-trust brainstorming sessions in a browser where everyone can “stick notes, sketch flows, and chat while editing” on the same computer across up to 12 tabs/windows treated as collaborators. Users enter through a local-only username + password profile screen and then select a UI persona role (Admin, Academic Affairs, Teacher) that simply changes visible menus and allowed actions for convenience (it is not a security boundary in a pure browser app). As a local UX safeguard, the app can pause sign-in for 15 minutes after 3 wrong password attempts for that profile, and it auto-signs-out a profile after 7 days of no successful sign-in based on a locally stored timestamp (both behaviors are resettable by clearing browser data). Each workspace contains a Canvas with a shape toolbar (rectangle, circle, arrow, connector lines, freehand pen) and a Sticky Note mode where each note is limited to 80 characters; bulk import supports up to 1,000 notes from CSV/JSON with a column-mapping Modal, row-level validation, and an error Table for rejected rows. Any canvas element or note supports a threaded comment Drawer with up to 50 replies per thread; comments allow @mentions from the workspace roster and generate in-app inbox items (badge count + Toast). A right-side Chat panel keeps the most recent 500 messages, supports keyword search, and posts system messages for key actions. Presence is shown via an avatar bar, colored cursors, and a “recent action” activity feed with timestamps and object links. To support structured collaboration beyond brainstorming, the workspace also includes a Mutual-Help board where users can post requests/offers using configurable forms (category, tags, time window, budget/compensation, urgency, attachments), with draft/edit/withdraw, pinning, and expiration (default 72 hours) applied when the app is open.

Built as an Angular + TypeScript single-page application, all logic runs fully offline with no network dependencies. UI state is managed with Angular services and RxJS stores, while cross-tab “collaboration” uses BroadcastChannel to sync presence, cursor positions, chat, and edit events with simple version counters and conflict prompts when two tabs edit the same object. LocalStorage holds lightweight preferences (theme, last-opened workspace, persona role, privacy masking toggle), while IndexedDB stores all workspaces, canvas objects, comment threads, chat logs, event telemetry, and attachment blobs (single attachment ≤ 20 MB). The app records local interaction events (views, clicks, creates/edits) and computes real-time KPIs—e.g., notes created per minute, comment response time, unresolved requests—showing alert Toasts when thresholds are crossed; a Web Worker performs aggregation and builds an offline “warehouse” of daily summaries for a simple reporting view. Workspaces auto-save every 10 seconds while open, retain up to 200 snapshots, and support one-click rollback. “Workspace packages” can be exported/imported for USB transfer using the File API (and File System Access API when available): each package is ≤ 200 MB, includes attachments and snapshots, and on import a same-name conflict prompts a choice to overwrite or create a copy. A Service Worker may be used to cache app assets for installable, reliable offline launches.
